cards.front: What is the purpose of creating a user in a database management system?
cards.back: To add a new user to the database and control their access to the data

cards.front: What are the different types of permissions that can be granted to a user in a database management system?
cards.back: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, DROP

cards.front: How to create a user in most database management systems?
cards.back: Using the CREATE USER command, followed by GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES TO THE NEW USER

cards.front: What is the GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES command used for?
cards.back: To grant all the necessary permissions to the user, allowing them to perform all database operations

cards.front: What is the purpose of the REVOKE command in a database management system?
cards.back: To remove or revoke existing permissions from a user, limiting their access to the data
